code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test07-OCT-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. HbA1c is low, making chronic hyperglycemia unlikely. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney dysfunction; further nephrological evaluation is recommended. No clear evidence of urinary tract infection.
